question: What are the steps to connect an IP control device to the TV?
documents: Connecting an IP control device to the TV. Settings General Network Expert Settings IP Remote You can connect an IP control device to the TV remotely to manage your IP device. To use this function, Power On with Mobile must be turned on. Turning this feature on may allow other IP control devices to control your TV. We recommend turning this feature on only if an authorized third-party custom controller is installed and configured specifically for a Samsung TV and if your Wi-Fi network is password protected. This function may not be supported depending on the model or geographical area.
response:
1. Go to Settings.
2. Navigate to General.
3. Select Network.
4. Choose Expert Settings.
5. Click on IP Remote.
6. Turn on the Power On with Mobile feature.
7. Ensure that an authorized third-party custom controller is installed and configured specifically for a Samsung TV.
8. Make sure your Wi-Fi network is password protected.

question: TV is making a humming noise. What should I do?
documents: I Can't Hear the Sound Clearly. When the TV has difficulties with sound, these steps may help resolve the problem.
response:
To address the issue of the TV making a humming noise, you can try the following steps:

1. Check the volume control of your TV and any external devices connected to it.
2. Reboot the connected device by disconnecting and then reconnecting its power cable.
3. Make sure that the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device.
4. If using a receiver that does not support Dolby Digital Plus, check and adjust the Digital Output Audio Format settings to Dolby Digital+ in the TV's sound settings.
5. Run a Start Sound Test in the Support or Self Diagnosis section to check for any distortion in the sound output.
6. If the issue persists, it is recommended to consult the TV's user manual or contact customer support for further assistance.

question: How do I use the accessibility Shortcuts menu?
documents: Running Accessibility Shortcuts. The Accessibility Shortcuts provide easy access to the accessibility functions. To display the Accessibility Shortcuts press and hold the Volume button for 1 second or more. You can easily turn on or turn off the functions such as Voice Guide Video Description High Contrast Enlarge Grayscale Color Inversion Learn TV Remote Learn Menu Screen Caption Multi-output Audio , etc. On the standard remote control, press the CC/VD button. Even if the Voice Guide is set to Off or muted, the voice guide for Accessibility Shortcuts is enabled. The menus may not appear depending on the model or geographical area.
response:
To use the Accessibility Shortcuts menu:
1. Press and hold the VOL button on the Samsung Smart Remote to open the Accessibility Shortcuts menu.
2. Once the menu is open, use the Directional and Select buttons to navigate.
3. Use the up and down directional buttons to move between menu items.
4. Press the Select button to turn on or turn off the function indicated by the menu where you are located.
5. Use the up and down directional buttons to move between options within a function and press Select to choose an option.
6. To close the menu, use the right directional button to get to the Close option and select it, or press the button.

question: How do I access the main accessibility menu to change Voice Guide settings?
documents: Step by Step instructions to run the options. Turning on Voice Guide Press and hold the VOL button on the remote. The Accessibility Shortcuts menu appears. Press the up or down directional buttons on the remote to move to and highlight the Voice Guide option. Press the Select button on the remote to turn Voice Guide on or off. Close the menu either by pressing on the remote or by pressing the right directional button to highlight Close , and then pressing the Select button.  Turning on Video Description Press and hold the VOL button on the remote. The Accessibility Shortcuts menu appears. Press the up or down directional buttons on the remote to move to and highlight the Video Description option. Press the Select button on the remote to turn Video Description on or off. Close the menu either by pressing on the remote or by pressing the right directional button to highlight Close , and then pressing the Select button. Learning TV Remote Press and hold the VOL button on the remote. The Accessibility Shortcuts menu appears. Press the up or down directional buttons on the remote to move to and highlight the Learn TV Remote option. Press the Select button on the remote. You are now in the e-Manual and specifically on the Learn TV Remote page. Press a button on the remote. The name and function of the button are heard. When you have finished and want to exit the e-Manual, press the button twice to go to the previous page. Accessing the main accessibility menu to change Voice Guide settings You can also go to an accessibility menu from the TV settings menu. This provides more options, for example, to change the speed of Voice Guide. The TV will not verbalize this menu unless Voice Guide is already turned on. Press the button. Press the left directional button until you reach Settings Press Select and a menu will open. Press the down directional button to reach General , and then press Select to open this menu. Use the directional buttons to go to the Accessibility menu, and then press Select to open this menu. The menu will appear with Voice Guide Settings being the first menu. Highlight Voice Guide Settings , and then press Select. A menu appears with the options to change Voice Guide and Volume Speed Pitch Select the menu using the directional buttons, and then press Select.
response:
To access the main accessibility menu to change Voice Guide settings, you can go to the TV settings menu. From there, follow these steps:

1. Press the button.
2. Press the left directional button until you reach Settings.
3. Press Select to open the Settings menu.
4. Use the down directional button to reach General and then press Select to open this menu.
5. Navigate to the Accessibility menu using the directional buttons and press Select to open this menu.
6. The menu will appear with Voice Guide Settings being the first menu.
7. Highlight Voice Guide Settings and then press Select.
8. A menu will appear with options to change Voice Guide and Volume Speed Pitch. Select the menu using the directional buttons and press Select.

question: Explain the procedure for cancelling Scheduled Viewing / Scheduled Recording?
documents: Cancelling a scheduled viewing. You have two ways to cancel a scheduled viewing. 1. Cancelling a scheduled viewing from the Guide Live TV Guide Move to the program you want to cancel scheduled viewing for, and then press the Select button. Move to Cancel Scheduled Viewing , and then press the Select button. When a pop-up message asking you to cancel the selected scheduled viewing appears, select Yes . The selected scheduled viewing is canceled and the screen returns to the Guide screen. 2. Cancelling a scheduled viewing from Smart Hub Live TV Schedule Manager Recordings Schedules Delete Press the button to open Smart Hub , and then press the left or right directional button to move to Live TV Press the up directional button to move to the top row, and then press the left or right directional button to move to Schedule Manager Recordings Press Select to open Schedule Manager Recordings Press the up directional button to move to the Schedules menu. Use the down directional button to move to the list of programs scheduled to view. Use the right directional button to move to the Delete option, and then press the Select button to delete the selected item. This function may not be supported depending on the model or geographical area.
response:
To cancel a scheduled viewing or scheduled recording, you have two options. 

1. Cancelling a scheduled viewing can be done from the Live TV Guide. Move to the program you want to cancel the viewing for and press the Select button. Then, move to Cancel Scheduled Viewing and press Select. A pop-up message will appear asking if you want to cancel the selected scheduled viewing, choose Yes. The scheduled viewing will be canceled and the screen will return to the Guide screen.

2. Cancelling a scheduled viewing or recording can also be done from Smart Hub. Press the button to open Smart Hub and then use the directional buttons to navigate to Live TV. Then, go to Schedule Manager Recordings and select it. Move to the Schedules menu and select the program you want to cancel. Use the right directional button to move to the Delete option, and then press Select to delete the selected item. This function may not be supported on all models or in all geographical areas.

question: How to change the Ambient Mode settings?
documents: Changing the content and settings for Ambient Mode. When you press the button in Ambient Mode , the Ambient Mode browser screen appears. Setting up the content for Ambient Mode The Ambient Mode browser screen displays content at the top and categories at the bottom. The selected content is played in Ambient Mode In the future, more content that you can set up in the Ambient Mode browser will be provided. You can select the following categories and content: Decor : Allows you to select beautiful screens. Info : Provides information such as weather, news headlines, and more. This function may not be supported depending on the geographical area. Photo : Allows you to set a picture stored in your mobile device as the wallpaper of the Ambient Mode screen. You can configure special layouts using your photos. To save photos from your mobile device to the TV and import them in Ambient Mode , use the SmartThings app on your mobile device.  Setting up the Ambient Mode details In the Ambient Mode browser screen, move the focus to , and then press the Select button. You can change the following settings: Brightness : Adjusts the screen brightness for Ambient Mode Color Tone : Adjusts the colors of the screen for Ambient Mode Auto Brightness : Changes the auto brightness setting for Ambient Mode When this function is set to Off , the brightness level of the TV screen is not automatically adjusted according to the ambient light level. Ambient Off Timer : Sets the time that the Ambient Mode screen turns off automatically. If there is no remote control input for the set time, the TV switches to the black screen state. Changing the background color of Ambient Mode To change the background color of Ambient Mode , move the focus to in the Ambient Mode browser screen, and then press the Select button. You can change the background color or pattern. Move the focus to a color or pattern you want, and then press the Select button. Take a picture of a wall using the SmartThings app on your mobile device to set it as the background of Ambient Mode This function may have a delay in image transmission and optimization depending on the network conditions.

question: I am getting error 'wireless router is not found'. How do I fix this?
documents: Troubleshooting Internet connectivity issues. Try Now After reading the following content, troubleshoot the Internet connection issue. If the problem persists, contact your Internet Service Provider. No network cable found Make sure that the LAN cable is plugged in on both ends. If it is plugged in, make sure that the router is turned on. If the router is on, try turning it off and then on. Wireless network connection failed If a selected wireless router is not found, go to Open Network Settings , and then select the correct router. Settings General Network Open Network Settings Unable to connect to a wireless router Check if the router is turned on. If it is, turn it off and then on. Enter the correct password if required.  IP auto setting failed Configure the settings in IP Settings Settings General Network Network Status IP Settings Make sure that the DHCP server is enabled on the router, and then unplug the router and plug it back in. Reset the router if required. For wireless connection, enter the correct password if required. Unable to connect to the network Check all IP Settings Settings General Network Network Status IP Settings After checking the DHCP server status (must be active) on the router, remove the LAN cable, and then connect it again. For wireless connection, enter the correct password if required. Connected to a local network, but not to the Internet Make sure that the Internet LAN cable is connected to the router's external LAN port. Check the DNS values in IP Settings Settings General Network Network Status IP Settings Network setup is complete, but unable to connect to the Internet If the problem persists, contact your Internet Service Provider.
response:
To fix the error 'wireless router is not found', you should follow these steps:

1. Go to Open Network Settings on your device.
2. Select the correct wireless router from the available networks.
3. If the router is still not found, manually enter the network name (SSID) by selecting Add Network.
4. If your wireless router has a WPS or PBC button, you can also try using the WPS option at the bottom of the list and pressing the WPS or PBC button on your router within 2 minutes to automatically connect the TV.

question: How do I do instant recording from the guide screen and Explain the steps how to do Schedule Recording from the guide screen?
documents: Using the instant and schedule recording options from the guide screen:. To record a program that is on now, open the program guide, move to the program, and then press and hold the Select button. You will get a pop-up menu. Use the up and down directional buttons to move to Record . Press select to record the program. You will be returned to the program guide. Recording automatically ends at the time when the program ends. To open the Guide and stop recording the program, press and hold the Select button. The menu is displayed and there is now a Stop Recording option instead of a Record option. Move to the Stop Recording option, and then press the Select button. To record a program that is on later, move to the program in the Guide , and then press Select. You will get a pop-up menu. Use the up and down directional button to move within this menu. Move to the Schedule Recording , and then press Select. You will hear a message saying the program has been set to record and then you will be returned to the program guide. The icon is placed visually next to the program title to show it is set to record. To find out if a program is set to record or to cancel a scheduled recording, press select on the program. If it is already set to record, then the option on the menu will be Cancel Scheduled Recording . You can press the Select button to cancel the scheduled recording or just press the button to go back to the program guide without cancelling the recording.
response:
To do an instant recording from the guide screen, select a live program from the Guide, press and hold the Select button, and then select Record from the pop-up menu to start recording immediately.

To schedule a recording from the guide screen, first move to the program in the Guide that you want to schedule recording for. Press the Select button, then select Schedule Recording from the pop-up menu. You will hear a message saying the program has been set to record, and an icon will be visually placed next to the program title to indicate it is set to record.

response:
If your TV screen has become black, you can try the following steps to troubleshoot the issue:

1. Confirm that the TV is receiving power and is turned on properly.
2. Check the video input connectors to ensure they are connected to the correct external device output connectors.
3. Make sure all connections are secure and there are no loose cables.
4. If the TV screen is still black, try adjusting the Picture Mode settings for brightness, contrast, sharpness, and color.
5. Make sure the Power Saving Mode is not enabled, as this could affect the brightness of the screen.

question: What are the ways to establish a wireless Internet connection?
documents: Connecting to your Internet network. Settings General Network Open Network Settings Try Now Connect to an available network. Establishing a wired Internet connection Settings General Network Open Network Settings Wired If you connect a LAN cable, the TV automatically accesses the Internet. Use a CAT 7 (*STP type) cable for the connection. * Shielded Twist Pair The TV will not be able to connect to the Internet if your network speed is below 10 Mbps. LAN  Establishing a wireless Internet connection Settings General Network Open Network Settings Wireless Make sure that you have the wireless router's SSID (name) and password settings before attempting to connect, and then follow the directions on the screen. The image on your TV may differ from the image above depending on the model and geographical area. If no wireless router is found, select Add Network at the bottom of the list and enter the network name (SSID). If your wireless router has a WPS or PBC button, select Use WPS at the bottom of the list, and then push the WPS or PBC button on your router within 2 minutes. The TV will connect automatically. Checking the Internet connection status Settings General Network Network Status Try Now View the current network and Internet status. Wireless Connection Select your wireless network. Refresh
response:
There are two ways to establish a wireless Internet connection:

1. Make sure you have the wireless router's SSID and password settings, then follow the directions on the screen to connect to the wireless network.
2. If no wireless router is found, you can select "Add Network" at the bottom of the list and enter the network name (SSID). Additionally, if your wireless router has a WPS or PBC button, you can select "Use WPS" at the bottom of the list and push the WPS or PBC button on your router within 2 minutes for automatic connection.

question: Can I change the background color of Ambient Mode?

question: How to change the current time and set the clock manually?
documents: Setting the current time. Settings General System Manager Time Try Now You can set the Clock manually or automatically. Once the Clock is set, you can view the current time on the TV anytime. You must reset the clock in the following cases: The power cable is disconnected and then connected. The Clock Mode is changed from Manual Auto The TV is not connected to the Internet. No broadcast signals are received. Setting the clock automatically Settings General System Manager Time Clock Clock Mode Auto This function works only when the TV is connected to the Internet or is receiving digital broadcasts through a connected antenna. The accuracy of the time information received may differ with the channel and signal. If you get your TV signal from a cable broadcast receiver/set-top box or a satellite receiver/satellite set-top box connected to an HDMI or Component port, you must set the current time manually.  Adjusting the clock for DST and time zone Settings General System Manager Time Clock Sets the right time by setting Daylight Savings Time (DST) and your local time zone. Time Zone Selects your time zone. This function is only available when the Clock Mode is set to Auto DST Automatically adjusts for Daylight Saving Time (DST). This function is only available when the Clock Mode is set to Auto Changing the current time Settings General System Manager Time Clock Time Offset Time Offset adjusts the time through a network connection. This function is only available if Clock Mode is set to Auto and the TV is connected to the Internet though a local area network. Time Offset adjusts the time if the TV fails to receive time information through normal digital broadcast signals. Setting the clock manually Settings General System Manager Time Clock Clock Mode Manual When Clock Mode is set to Manual , you can directly enter the current time. Select the Date and Time by using the directional buttons on the remote control.
response:
To change the current time and set the clock manually, follow these steps:

1. Go to Settings on the TV.
2. Navigate to General > System Manager > Time.
3. Select Clock.
4. Set Clock Mode to Manual.
5. Use the directional buttons on the remote control to select the Date and Time and directly enter the current time.

question: I want to use HDMI UHD Color. How can I use it?

question: Anynet+ device won't play. What should I do?
documents: Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) Isn't Working. When Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) isn't working, these steps may help resolve the problem. The problem Try this! Anynet+ does not work. I want to start Anynet+. The connected device is not displayed. The TV audio is not being played through the receiver. Confirm that the device is an Anynet+ device. The Anynet+ system supports Anynet+ devices only. Check if the power cord of the Anynet+ device is properly connected. Check the cable connections of the Anynet+ device. Go to Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) and see if Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) has been set to Settings General External Device Manager Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) Anynet+ may not function when some other functions are active, including Channel Search, Smart Hub, Initial Setup, etc. If you have disconnected and then reconnected the HDMI cable, scan for devices again or turn your TV off and on. Move the focus to the Anynet+ device at Source , and then press the up directional button. Check if the Anynet+ device is properly connected to the TV, and then select Anynet+ (HDMI- CEC) menu to see if Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) is set to Settings General External Device Manager Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) Set Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) Off Settings General External Device Manager Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) You cannot use the remote control when the TV is configuring Anynet+ or switching to a viewing mode. Use the remote control after the TV has completed the Anynet+ configuration or has switched to a viewing mode. Check whether the device supports Anynet+. Anynet+ devices must be connected to the TV using an HDMI cable. Make sure the device is connected to your TV with an HDMI cable. Some HDMI cables may not support Anynet+. Check whether the HDMI cable is properly connected. Go to Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) and see if Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) has been set to Settings General External Device Manager Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) Scan for Anynet+ devices again. If the connection is terminated because there has been a power failure or the HDMI cable has been disconnected, please scan for the device again. Make sure that the receiver is connected to the TV's HDMI (ARC) port with an HDMI cable. Confirm that the receiver is HDMI (ARC) compatible. If the receiver is not HDMI (ARC) compatible, connect the TV and receiver with an optical cable.
response:
If the Anynet+ device won't play, you should try the following steps to resolve the issue:

1. Confirm that the device is an Anynet+ device connected to the TV with an HDMI cable.
2. Check if the power cord of the Anynet+ device is properly connected and the cable connections are secure.
3. Make sure that Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC) has been set correctly in the TV settings under Settings > General > External Device Manager > Anynet+ (HDMI-CEC).
4. Check if any other functions on the TV, such as Channel Search, Smart Hub, or Initial Setup, are active, as these may interfere with Anynet+ functionality.
5. If you have disconnected and reconnected the HDMI cable, scan for devices again or turn the TV off and on to refresh the connection.
6. Ensure that the HDMI cable used is compatible with Anynet+ and properly connected to both the device and the TV.
7. Wait for the Anynet+ configuration process to complete before using the remote control to operate the device.

By following these steps, you should be able to troubleshoot and resolve the issue of the Anynet+ device not playing.

question: How do I Cancel Scheduled Viewing / Cancel Scheduled Recording?
documents: Cancelling a scheduled viewing. You have two ways to cancel a scheduled viewing. 1. Cancelling a scheduled viewing from the Guide Live TV Guide Move to the program you want to cancel scheduled viewing for, and then press the Select button. Move to Cancel Scheduled Viewing , and then press the Select button. When a pop-up message asking you to cancel the selected scheduled viewing appears, select Yes . The selected scheduled viewing is canceled and the screen returns to the Guide screen. 2. Cancelling a scheduled viewing from Smart Hub Live TV Schedule Manager Recordings Schedules Delete Press the button to open Smart Hub , and then press the left or right directional button to move to Live TV Press the up directional button to move to the top row, and then press the left or right directional button to move to Schedule Manager Recordings Press Select to open Schedule Manager Recordings Press the up directional button to move to the Schedules menu. Use the down directional button to move to the list of programs scheduled to view. Use the right directional button to move to the Delete option, and then press the Select button to delete the selected item. This function may not be supported depending on the model or geographical area.
response:
To cancel a scheduled viewing or scheduled recording, you can follow these steps:

1. Cancelling a scheduled viewing from the Guide Live TV Guide:
   - Move to the program you want to cancel scheduled viewing for and press the Select button.
   - Move to "Cancel Scheduled Viewing" and press the Select button.
   - Select "Yes" when a pop-up message asking you to cancel the selected scheduled viewing appears.

2. Cancelling a scheduled viewing from Smart Hub Live TV Schedule Manager Recordings Schedules:
   - Press the button to open Smart Hub, then use the directional buttons to navigate to Live TV.
   - Move to Schedule Manager Recordings and press Select.
   - Navigate to the Schedules menu and select the program you want to cancel.
   - Move to the Delete option and press the Select button to delete the selected item.

Those are the steps to cancel a scheduled viewing or scheduled recording on your device.

question: How do I start recording?

To record a program that is on later, move to the program in the Guide , and then press Select. You will get a pop-up menu. Use the up and down directional button to move within this menu. Move to the Schedule Recording , and then press Select. You will hear a message saying the program has been set to record and then you will be returned to the program guide. The icon is placed visually next to the program title to show it is set to record. To find out if a program is set to record or to cancel a scheduled recording, press select on the program. question: How do I fix the poor color issue?
documents: There Is a Problem with the Picture. When the TV has trouble with the picture, these steps may help resolve the problem.
response:
To fix the poor color issue on your TV, you can follow these steps:

1. Run the Start Picture Test to confirm if the issue is caused by the TV itself.
2. Adjust the Picture Mode settings in the Picture menu, specifically the Brightness, Sharpness, and Color settings.
3. Check if Power Saving Mode has been enabled in the General Eco Solution settings and disable it if necessary.
4. If resetting the picture settings is needed, go to the Picture Expert Settings and select Reset Picture.
5. If you are using AV or analog equipment, make sure to connect the adapter from the Component (Blue) input port on the TV to the AV (Yellow) input port.
6. Use HDMI Black Level in the General External Device Manager settings to adjust the black level if necessary.

By following these steps, you should be able to address the poor color issue on your TV.

question: Connected device is not displaying. How can I fix this?

question: How to connect to the TV via the SmartThings app?
documents: Connecting to the TV via the SmartThings app. Turn on the TV. Start the SmartThings app on your mobile device. response:
To connect to the TV via the SmartThings app, you need to follow these steps:

1. Start by turning on the TV.
2. Open the SmartThings app on your mobile device.
3. Tap on "Add Device" on the dashboard of the SmartThings app.
4. The app will search for connectable TVs. Select the model of your TV from the search results.
5. Enter the PIN number displayed on the TV screen to register the TV with your Samsung account.
6. Once the TV is registered, you can use the SmartThings app to control the TV.
7. If your TV is not found on your mobile device, turn both devices off and on, then try again.
8. If you have more than one TV, you can easily select the TV you want by setting different TV names in the settings.

question: I facing low quality picture issue. How to fix this?
documents: There Is a Problem with the Picture. When the TV has trouble with the picture, these steps may help resolve the problem.
response:
To fix the low quality picture issue, you can try the following steps:

1. Adjust the Picture Mode and Expert Settings by going to Settings > Picture Mode and adjusting settings such as Brightness, Sharpness, and Color.
2. Make sure the Power Saving Mode is disabled by going to Settings > General > Eco Solution > Power Saving Mode.
3. Reset the picture settings by going to Picture > Expert Settings > Reset Picture.
4. Check the connections of external devices to ensure they are properly connected and functioning.
5. If using AV or analog equipment, disconnect the adapter from the Component input port on the TV and connect it to the AV input port.
6. If the issue persists, you may need to contact Samsung's Call Center for further assistance.

question: How do I set the current time on the TV?
documents: Setting the current time. Settings General System Manager Time Try Now You can set the Clock manually or automatically. Once the Clock is set, you can view the current time on the TV anytime. You must reset the clock in the following cases: The power cable is disconnected and then connected. The Clock Mode is changed from Manual Auto The TV is not connected to the Internet. No broadcast signals are received. Setting the clock automatically Settings General System Manager Time Clock Clock Mode Auto This function works only when the TV is connected to the Internet or is receiving digital broadcasts through a connected antenna. The accuracy of the time information received may differ with the channel and signal. If you get your TV signal from a cable broadcast receiver/set-top box or a satellite receiver/satellite set-top box connected to an HDMI or Component port, you must set the current time manually.  Adjusting the clock for DST and time zone Settings General System Manager Time Clock Sets the right time by setting Daylight Savings Time (DST) and your local time zone. Time Zone Selects your time zone. This function is only available when the Clock Mode is set to Auto DST Automatically adjusts for Daylight Saving Time (DST). This function is only available when the Clock Mode is set to Auto Changing the current time Settings General System Manager Time Clock Time Offset Time Offset adjusts the time through a network connection. This function is only available if Clock Mode is set to Auto and the TV is connected to the Internet though a local area network. Time Offset adjusts the time if the TV fails to receive time information through normal digital broadcast signals. Setting the clock manually Settings General System Manager Time Clock Clock Mode Manual When Clock Mode is set to Manual , you can directly enter the current time. Select the Date and Time by using the directional buttons on the remote control.
response:
To set the current time on the TV, you can do so manually or automatically. 

1. To set it automatically, go to Settings > General > System Manager > Time > Clock Mode > Auto. This function works only when the TV is connected to the Internet or receiving digital broadcasts through a connected antenna.

2. To set it manually, go to Settings > General > System Manager > Time > Clock Mode > Manual. Select the Date and Time by using the directional buttons on the remote control to directly enter the current time. 

Remember to reset the clock in the following cases: when the power cable is disconnected and then connected, when the Clock Mode is changed from Manual to Auto, when the TV is not connected to the Internet, or when no broadcast signals are being received.

question: I am getting error not connected to network. How do I fix this?
documents: Troubleshooting Internet connectivity issues. Try Now After reading the following content, troubleshoot the Internet connection issue. If the problem persists, contact your Internet Service Provider. No network cable found Make sure that the LAN cable is plugged in on both ends. If it is plugged in, make sure that the router is turned on. If the router is on, try turning it off and then on. Wireless network connection failed If a selected wireless router is not found, go to Open Network Settings , and then select the correct router. Settings General Network Open Network Settings Unable to connect to a wireless router Check if the router is turned on. If it is, turn it off and then on. Enter the correct password if required.  IP auto setting failed Configure the settings in IP Settings Settings General Network Network Status IP Settings Make sure that the DHCP server is enabled on the router, and then unplug the router and plug it back in. Reset the router if required. For wireless connection, enter the correct password if required. Unable to connect to the network Check all IP Settings Settings General Network Network Status IP Settings After checking the DHCP server status (must be active) on the router, remove the LAN cable, and then connect it again. For wireless connection, enter the correct password if required. Connected to a local network, but not to the Internet Make sure that the Internet LAN cable is connected to the router's external LAN port. Check the DNS values in IP Settings Settings General Network Network Status IP Settings Network setup is complete, but unable to connect to the Internet If the problem persists, contact your Internet Service Provider.

question: What can I do if picture is good but there is no sound with it ?
documents: Picture and Sound. You can change the settings for the picture and the sound according to your preference.
response:
If the picture is good but there is no sound, you can try the following steps:

1. Check the volume control of your TV and the external device (such as a cable box or DVD player) connected to your TV.
2. Make sure the external device's audio output option is correctly set (e.g. change the cable box's audio option to HDMI if it's connected using an HDMI cable).
3. If listening to computer sound, connect an external speaker to the computer's audio output connector.
4. Ensure that the TV's headphone jack is not plugged into anything.
5. Reboot the connected device by disconnecting and then reconnecting its power cable.
6. Check the Digital Output Audio Format on the TV settings and ensure it is set to Dolby Digital+ (change if needed).
7. If using a receiver that does not support Dolby Digital Plus, you may hear no sound, so adjust the setting accordingly.
8. Make sure the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device.
9. For antenna or cable connections, check the signal information as a low signal level may cause sound distortions.
10. If these steps do not resolve the issue, run the Start Sound Test in the TV settings to diagnose further.
overall_supported_explanation: The response is well-supported by the documents, specifically Document 1, which provides detailed steps for what to do if there is no sound despite having a good picture. Document 2 also generally supports the advice on troubleshooting sound issues on a TV.

question: I am getting distorted picture on TV. How to fix this issue?
documents: There Is a Problem with the Picture. When the TV has trouble with the picture, these steps may help resolve the problem.
response:
To fix the issue of a distorted picture on your TV, you can try the following steps:

1. Check the video input connectors to ensure they are connected properly to the correct external device output connectors.
2. Confirm that the Pb, Pr, and Y jacks of a component cable are plugged into their proper connectors if your TV is connected to an external device that way.
3. Adjust settings such as Backlight, Contrast, Brightness, Sharpness, Color, and Tint to improve picture quality.
4. Use the Auto Motion Plus function to address ghosting or blurring on the screen.
5. Ensure that the Component cables are connected to the correct jacks and are not loose.
6. If the issue persists, consider resetting the picture settings or contacting Samsung's Call Center for assistance.

question: How do I troubleshoot sound issues ?
documents: Testing the sound. Settings Support Self Diagnosis Start Sound Test Try Now If the TV plays the Start Sound Test melody without distortion, there may be a problem with an external device or the broadcast signal's strength. The problem Try this! There is no sound or the sound is too low at maximum volume. The picture is good but there is no sound. No sound is heard. The speakers are making an odd sound. Check the volume control of your TV, and then check the volume control of the external device (cable box or satellite box, DVD, Blu-ray, etc.) connected to your TV. Set Sound Output TV Speaker Settings Sound Sound Output If you are using an external device, check the device's audio output option. For example, you may need to change your cable box's audio option to HDMI if the box connected to your TV is using an HDMI cable. To listen to computer sound, connect an external speaker to the computer's audio output connector. If your TV has a headphone jack, make sure there is nothing plugged into it. Reboot the connected device by disconnecting and then reconnecting the device's power cable. Check whether the Digital Output Audio Format is set to Dolby Digital+ If you are using a receiver that does not support Dolby Digital Plus, you will hear no sound when you select Dolby Digital+ Settings Sound Expert Settings Digital Output Audio Format Dolby Digital+ Run Start Sound Test Settings Support Self Diagnosis Start Sound Test Make sure that the audio cable is connected to the correct audio output connector on the external device. For antenna or cable connections, check the signal information. A low signal level may cause sound distortions.
response:
To troubleshoot sound issues on your TV, follow these steps:
1. Check the volume control on your TV and the external device connected to your TV.
2. Ensure that the audio output option on the external device is set correctly (e.g. HDMI).
3. If using a computer, connect an external speaker to the computer's audio output connector.
4. Make sure there is nothing plugged into the headphone jack on your TV.
5. Reboot the connected device by disconnecting and then reconnecting its power cable.
6. Check if the Digital Output Audio Format is set to Dolby Digital+ and adjust if necessary.
7. If using a receiver that does not support Dolby Digital Plus, adjust the settings accordingly.
8. Make sure the audio cable is connected to the correct output connector on the external device.
9. Check the signal strength if using antenna or cable connections, as a low signal level may cause sound distortions.

question: In which cases do I need to reset the clock time?
documents: Setting the current time. Settings General System Manager Time Try Now You can set the Clock manually or automatically. Once the Clock is set, you can view the current time on the TV anytime. You must reset the clock in the following cases: The power cable is disconnected and then connected. The Clock Mode is changed from Manual Auto The TV is not connected to the Internet. No broadcast signals are received. Setting the clock automatically Settings General System Manager Time Clock Clock Mode Auto This function works only when the TV is connected to the Internet or is receiving digital broadcasts through a connected antenna. The accuracy of the time information received may differ with the channel and signal. If you get your TV signal from a cable broadcast receiver/set-top box or a satellite receiver/satellite set-top box connected to an HDMI or Component port, you must set the current time manually.  Adjusting the clock for DST and time zone Settings General System Manager Time Clock Sets the right time by setting Daylight Savings Time (DST) and your local time zone. Time Zone Selects your time zone. This function is only available when the Clock Mode is set to Auto DST Automatically adjusts for Daylight Saving Time (DST). This function is only available when the Clock Mode is set to Auto Changing the current time Settings General System Manager Time Clock Time Offset Time Offset adjusts the time through a network connection. This function is only available if Clock Mode is set to Auto and the TV is connected to the Internet though a local area network. Time Offset adjusts the time if the TV fails to receive time information through normal digital broadcast signals. Setting the clock manually Settings General System Manager Time Clock Clock Mode Manual When Clock Mode is set to Manual , you can directly enter the current time. Select the Date and Time by using the directional buttons on the remote control.
response:
You need to reset the clock time in the following cases:
1. The power cable is disconnected and then connected.
2. The Clock Mode is changed from Manual to Auto.
3. The TV is not connected to the Internet.
4. No broadcast signals are received.

question: What can I do if TV is not connecting to the network?
documents: Troubleshooting Internet Connectivity Issues. If your TV won't connect to the Internet, try the solutions below.
response:
If the TV is not connecting to the network, you can try the following steps:
1. Confirm that your wireless modem/router is on and connected to the Internet.
2. If using a wired connection, make sure to use a CAT 7 (*STP type) cable for the connection.
3. Make sure you have the wireless router's SSID and password settings before attempting to connect wirelessly.
4. If no wireless router is found, select Add Network and enter the network name (SSID).
5. If your wireless router has a WPS or PBC button, select Use WPS and push the WPS or PBC button on your router within 2 minutes for automatic connection.
overall_supported_explanation: The response is well-supported by the documents. question: Please explain the procedure how to edit recording time and Explain the procedure for cancelling Scheduled Viewing / Scheduled Recording?
documents: Managing the schedule recording list. Live TV Recordings Schedules You can change scheduled recording information or cancel scheduled recordings. You can schedule up to 30 recordings or viewings at once using Schedule Recording and Schedule Viewing Setting the Schedule Recording Time Live TV Recordings Schedules Edit You can set the start and end times for a schedule recording. Canceling a schedule recording Live TV Recordings Schedules Delete Checking scheduled recordings Live TV Recordings Schedules View Details
response:
To edit recording time, follow these steps:

1. Go to Live TV Schedule Manager Recordings Schedules.
2. Select the program for which you want to edit the recording time.
3. Choose the Edit option.
4. Set the start and end times for the scheduled recording. Note that the start time can be set up to 10 minutes before the program starts, and the end time can be set up to 10 minutes after the program ends.

To cancel a Scheduled Viewing or Scheduled Recording, follow these steps:

1. For cancelling a scheduled viewing:
   - From the Guide: Move to the program you want to cancel the scheduled viewing for and press the Select button. Then move to Cancel Scheduled Viewing and press Select. Confirm the cancellation when prompted.
   - From the Smart Hub: Open Smart Hub, navigate to Live TV, then Schedule Manager Recordings. Select the program you want to cancel from the list of scheduled viewings and choose the Delete option.

2. For cancelling a scheduled recording:
   - From Live TV Recordings Schedules: You can cancel a scheduled recording by selecting the program and choosing the Cancel option.

By following these steps, you can efficiently edit recording time and cancel Scheduled Viewing or Scheduled Recording.

question: How to cancel sheduled viewing?

question: Please provide some instructions on how to schedule program?
documents: Setting Up a Schedule Viewing. Configure the TV to show a specific channel or program at a specific time and date. response:
To schedule a program on your TV, follow these steps:

1. Ensure the TV's clock is set correctly in Settings > General > System Manager > Time > Clock.
2. On the Guide screen, select the program you would like to view.
3. Press and hold the Select button, then choose "Schedule Viewing" from the pop-up menu that appears.
4. Alternatively, while watching TV, press the Select button to bring up the Program Info window.
5. Use the left or right directional buttons to select a broadcasted program, then press Select.
6. Select "Schedule Viewing" to schedule watching the program at a specified time and date.
7. For users in the U.S.A and Canada, navigate to Live TV Schedule Manager > Add Viewing to set the values for Antenna Channel, Repeat Date, and Start Time, then save.
8. Remember to check the current time in Settings > General > System Manager > Time > Clock.
*Note: This function may not be available on all TV models or in certain geographical areas.*

question: I want  to adjust the screen brightness. Can you explian about that?
documents: Reducing the energy consumption of the TV. Settings General Eco Solution Try Now You can adjust the brightness level of the TV, reduce overall power consumption, and prevent overheating. Ambient Light Detection Try Now Automatically adjusts the brightness level of the TV, according to the ambient light level, to reduce power consumption. If Ambient Light Detection has adjusted the screen brightness to a too bright or dark level, you can select Minimum Backlight to manually adjust the minimum screen brightness. Minimum Backlight Try Now When Ambient Light Detection is turned on, you can manually adjust the minimum brightness of the TV screen. This function acts only when the value is less than the setting in Settings Picture Expert Settings Backlight Power Saving Mode Try Now Allows you to select a brightness setting from the list to reduce the TV's power consumption. Motion Lighting Try Now Adjusts the brightness in response to on-screen movements to reduce power consumption. Auto Power Off Try Now Automatically turns off the TV to reduce unnecessary power consumption if there is no operation for 4 hours.
response:
To adjust the screen brightness of the TV, you have a few options available:

1. Ambient Light Detection: This feature automatically adjusts the brightness level of the TV according to the ambient light level to reduce power consumption. If the brightness level is not to your liking, you can manually adjust the minimum screen brightness with the "Minimum Backlight" setting.

2. Backlight Power Saving Mode: This feature allows you to select a brightness setting from a list to reduce the TV's power consumption.

3. Motion Lighting: Adjusts the brightness in response to on-screen movements to reduce power consumption.

4. Auto Power Off: Automatically turns off the TV to reduce unnecessary power consumption if there is no operation for 4 hours.

Additionally, when the TV is in Ambient Mode, the brightness is automatically adjusted by the illuminance sensor based on the surroundings. There are different states within Ambient Mode such as the getting dark state and the black screen state, each with specific brightness adjustments according to the surroundings. You can also change the auto brightness setting for Ambient Mode to suit your preferences.

question: How to establish a wireless Internet connection?
documents: Connecting to your Internet network. Settings General Network Open Network Settings Try Now Connect to an available network. Establishing a wired Internet connection Settings General Network Open Network Settings Wired If you connect a LAN cable, the TV automatically accesses the Internet. Use a CAT 7 (*STP type) cable for the connection. * Shielded Twist Pair The TV will not be able to connect to the Internet if your network speed is below 10 Mbps. LAN  Establishing a wireless Internet connection Settings General Network Open Network Settings Wireless Make sure that you have the wireless router's SSID (name) and password settings before attempting to connect, and then follow the directions on the screen. The image on your TV may differ from the image above depending on the model and geographical area. If no wireless router is found, select Add Network at the bottom of the list and enter the network name (SSID). If your wireless router has a WPS or PBC button, select Use WPS at the bottom of the list, and then push the WPS or PBC button on your router within 2 minutes. The TV will connect automatically. Checking the Internet connection status Settings General Network Network Status Try Now View the current network and Internet status. Wireless Connection Select your wireless network. Refresh
response:
To establish a wireless Internet connection on your TV, follow these steps:

1. Make sure you have the wireless router's SSID and password settings.
2. Access the settings menu on your TV and navigate to General > Network > Open Network Settings > Wireless.
3. If your wireless router is not found, select "Add Network" at the bottom of the list and enter the network name (SSID).
4. If your wireless router has a WPS or PBC button, select "Use WPS" at the bottom of the list and push the WPS or PBC button on your router within 2 minutes.
5. Your TV will then automatically connect to the wireless network.
6. If you encounter any issues, refer to the Troubleshooting section for solutions or contact your Internet Service Provider.

question: How do I stop recording?
documents: Using the instant and schedule recording options from the guide screen:. To record a program that is on later, move to the program in the Guide , and then press Select. You will get a pop-up menu. Use the up and down directional button to move within this menu. Move to the Schedule Recording , and then press Select. You will hear a message saying the program has been set to record and then you will be returned to the program guide. The icon is placed visually next to the program title to show it is set to record. To find out if a program is set to record or to cancel a scheduled recording, press select on the program. If it is already set to record, then the option on the menu will be Cancel Scheduled Recording . You can press the Select button to cancel the scheduled recording or just press the button to go back to the program guide without cancelling the recording.

question: Can you please explain how to schedule recording?
documents: Recording programs. Using the instant and schedule recording options from the guide screen Instant Recording Select a live program from the Guide , press and hold the Select button, and then select Record from the pop- up menu to start recording immediately. Schedule Recording Select a program scheduled to be broadcast, press the Select button, and then select Schedule Recording set up a scheduled recording of the scheduled program. Using the instant and schedule recording options while watching a program Instant Recording If you select Record after pressing the button while watching a broadcast, recording starts. Schedule Recording Press the Select button while watching a program to display the program info window. Use the left or right directional button to select a scheduled program. Press the Select button, and then select Schedule Recording to set up a scheduled recording of the scheduled program.  Scheduling a video recording after entering the date and time Live TV Recordings Schedules Add Recording To schedule a recording, set the values for Antenna Channel Repeat Date Start Time , and End Time of the broadcast program to record, and then select Save Check the current time in Settings General System Manager Time Clock
response:
To schedule a recording, you can follow these steps:

1. Using the instant and schedule recording options from the guide screen:
   - Instant Recording: Select a live program from the Guide, press and hold the Select button, then select Record from the pop-up menu to start recording immediately.
   - Schedule Recording: Select a program scheduled to be broadcast, press the Select button, then select Schedule Recording to set up a scheduled recording of the program.

2. Using the instant and schedule recording options while watching a program:
   - Instant Recording: Press the Record button while watching a broadcast to start recording.
   - Schedule Recording: Press the Select button while watching a program to display the program info window. Use the directional buttons to select a scheduled program, press Select, then select Schedule Recording to set up a scheduled recording.

3. Scheduling a video recording after entering the date and time:
   - Live TV Recordings Schedules: Add Recording by setting values for Antenna Channel, Repeat Date, Start Time, and End Time of the program to record, then select Save.

You can also manage your schedule recording list by changing scheduled recording information, canceling scheduled recordings, or checking scheduled recordings in the Live TV Recordings Schedules menu. Additionally, you can set the start and end times for a schedule recording, adjust the start time up to 10 minutes before the program starts, and the end time up to 10 minutes after the program ends. Lastly, you can customize the schedule recording start by setting the option to start recording early or continue recording after the broadcast end time in the program guide in the Live TV Recordings Settings menu.

question: What can I do when wireless network signal is too weak?

response:
When the wireless network signal is too weak, you can try the following steps:
1. Confirm your wireless modem/router is on and connected to the Internet.
2. Make sure there are no physical obstructions blocking the wireless signal.
3. Move the router closer to the TV to improve signal strength.
4. Consider using a Wi-Fi range extender to boost the signal strength in the area where the TV is located.

If the problem persists, contact your Internet Service Provider for further assistance.

question: Wireless network signal is too weak. How can I fix this?
documents: The TV Won't Connect to the Internet. When the TV has difficulties connecting to the Internet, these steps may help resolve the problem. The problem Try this! The TV cannot connect to your network or apps (for Internet compatible models only). The wireless network Confirm your wireless modem/router is on and connected to the Internet. connection failed. The wireless network signal is too weak.
response:
To fix the issue of a weak wireless network signal, you can try the following steps:

1. Confirm that your wireless modem/router is on and connected to the internet.
2. Ensure that the router is not being obstructed by any physical barriers and is placed in a central location for better coverage.
3. Consider moving the router closer to the TV to strengthen the signal.
4. If possible, upgrade to a dual-band router for better signal strength.
5. Reduce interference from other devices by placing them farther away from the router.
6. Avoid placing the router near appliances or devices that may cause signal interference, such as microwaves or cordless phones.

question: What can I do if I hear no sound?
documents: I Can't Hear the Sound Clearly. When the TV has difficulties with sound, these steps may help resolve the problem.
response:
If you hear no sound, you can try the following troubleshooting steps:

1. Check the volume control of your TV and external device.
2. Make sure the audio output option on the external device is correctly set.
3. If using an HDMI cable, ensure the cable box's audio option is set to HDMI.
4. Connect external speakers to the computer's audio output connector for computer sound.
5. Make sure nothing is plugged into the TV's headphone jack.
6. Reboot the connected device by disconnecting and then reconnecting the power cable.
7. Check if the Digital Output Audio Format is set to Dolby Digital+.
8. Check the audio cable connection on the external device.
9. Check the signal information for antenna or cable connections for sound distortions.
10. It is recommended to use an AV receiver for better audio quality.
overall_supported_explanation: The response overall is supported by the documents, as they provide specific troubleshooting steps and general advice regarding sound issues on TVs and external devices. All provided response steps closely match the advice given in the documents around checking volume, cable settings, external device settings, and connections.
relevance_explanation: Documents 1 and 2 contain useful information for answering the question regarding what to do if there is no sound. Document 1 offers troubleshooting steps for issues related to TV sound, such as checking volume controls, ensuring correct audio output settings, and reconnecting power cables. Document 2 discusses how to configure audio devices, like using an AV receiver for better quality, and settings for digital audio outputs.

question: How do I fix the screen color issues?
documents: Inverting the screen color. Color Inversion is on, some Accessibility menus are not available. This function may not be supported depending on the model or geographical area.
response:
To fix screen color issues on your TV, you can follow these steps:

1. Run the Start Picture Test in the Settings Support Self Diagnosis menu to determine if the problem is caused by the TV.
2. If the colors on your TV screen are not correct or the black and white colors are off, run the Start Picture Test again.
3. Check and confirm that the video input connections are correctly plugged into the external device video output connectors.
4. Adjust the picture settings in the Picture Expert Settings such as Backlight, Contrast, Brightness, Sharpness, Color, and Tint (G/R) if the colors on your TV are too dark or bright.
5. If you notice ghosting or blurring on the screen, use the Auto Motion Plus Settings function to resolve the issue.
6. If the screen color is poor, not bright enough, or there is a dotted line on the edge of the screen, go to Picture settings and adjust Picture Mode, Brightness, Sharpness, and Color settings.
7. Try resetting the picture in the Picture Expert Settings or change the Picture Size in the Picture settings.
8. If using AV or analog equipment, make sure the adapter is connected to the correct input port on the TV.
9. If needed, you can also change the color of the TV screen to black and white by enabling the Grayscale mode in the Settings General Accessibility menu.

question: What are the steps to delete the recorded program?

The program will be deleted and you will be returned to the list of Recordings

question: How do I stop recording and Where do I find information about my program?
documents: Using the instant and schedule recording options from the guide screen:. Recording automatically ends at the time when the program ends. To open the Guide and stop recording the program, press and hold the Select button. The menu is displayed and there is now a Stop Recording option instead of a Record option. Move to the Stop Recording option, and then press the Select button. To record a program that is on later, move to the program in the Guide , and then press Select. You will get a pop-up menu. Use the up and down directional button to move within this menu. Move to the Schedule Recording , and then press Select. You will hear a message saying the program has been set to record and then you will be returned to the program guide. question: How to delete channels from favorite list?

1. Go to the Picture settings on your TV.
2. Adjust the Picture Mode, Brightness, Sharpness, and Color settings.
3. Check if Power Saving Mode has been enabled and disable it.
4. Try resetting the picture settings to default.
5. Change the Picture Size settings to "16:9 Standard".
6. If you are using AV or analog equipment, disconnect the adapter from the Component (Blue) input port on the TV and connect it to the AV (Yellow) input port. 
7. Check whether Grayscale is set to "Settings General Accessibility Grayscale".

If the issue persists after trying these steps, contact Samsung's Call Center for further assistance.

question: How to connect to Internet network?
documents: Connecting to your Internet network. Settings General Network Open Network Settings Try Now Connect to an available network. Establishing a wired Internet connection Settings General Network Open Network Settings Wired If you connect a LAN cable, the TV automatically accesses the Internet. Use a CAT 7 (*STP type) cable for the connection. * Shielded Twist Pair The TV will not be able to connect to the Internet if your network speed is below 10 Mbps. The image on your TV may differ from the image above depending on the model and geographical area. If no wireless router is found, select Add Network at the bottom of the list and enter the network name (SSID). Checking the Internet connection status Settings General Network Network Status Try Now View the current network and Internet status. Wireless Connection Select your wireless network. Refresh
response:
To connect to the Internet network, you can do so either through a wired connection or a wireless connection on your TV.

For a wired connection:
1. Connect a LAN cable to your TV and the router.
2. Use a CAT 7 (*STP type) cable for the connection.
3. Ensure that the network speed is at least 10 Mbps for the TV to connect to the Internet.

For a wireless connection:
1. Make sure you have the wireless router's SSID and password settings.
2. Access the network settings on your TV and select your wireless network.
3. If the router is not found, you can manually add the network or use the WPS button on the router if available.
4. Enter the correct password if required.

Once you have connected to the network, you can troubleshoot any connectivity issues using the network settings on your TV. If the problem persists, you may need to contact your Internet Service Provider for assistance.

question: What are the connection notes for HDMI?
documents: Connecting with an HDMI cable. HDMI IN
response:
The connection notes for HDMI are:
1. Use recommended types of HDMI cables: High-Speed HDMI Cable and High-Speed HDMI Cable with Ethernet.
2. Use an HDMI cable with a thickness of 0.66 inches (17 mm) or less.
3. Using a non-certified HDMI cable may result in a blank screen or a connection error.
4. Some HDMI cables and devices may not be compatible with the TV due to different HDMI specifications.
5. This TV does not support HDMI Ethernet Channel.
6. Use a cable shorter than 10 feet (3 m) for the best UHD viewing quality.
7. Many computer graphics adaptors do not have HDMI ports, but have DVI ports instead. If your PC does not support HDMI video out, connect your PC with an HDMI-DVI cable.

question: 'Mode not supported' appears on screen. How to fix this?
documents: My Computer Won't Connect. When the TV has difficulties connecting to a PC, these steps may help resolve the problem. The problem Try this! The Not Set your PC's output resolution so it matches a resolution supported by the TV. message appears. The video is OK but there is no audio. Select Source to confirm that the correct input source has been selected. If the TV is not connected to a cable box or satellite box, run Auto Program to search for channels. Settings Broadcasting Auto Program Confirm that the coaxial cable is securely connected to the TV. Run Start Setup Auto Program Settings General Start Setup Settings Broadcasting Auto Program Go to Caption Settings and change the Caption Mode Settings General Accessibility Caption Settings Caption Mode Some channels may not have caption data. The compression of the video content may cause picture distortions. This is especially true with fast moving pictures from sports programs and action movies. A weak signal can cause picture distortions. This is not a problem with the TV. If you are using an HDMI connection, check the audio output setting on your PC. If you are using an HDMI-to-DVI cable, a separate audio cable is required. Note that the HDMI-to-DVI connection is only supported by the HDMI (DVI) port and this port does not transmit audio. To listen to the computer sound, connect external speakers to the audio output connection of the computer.
response:
To fix the issue of 'Mode not supported' appearing on the screen, you can try the following steps:

1. Check and set your PC's output resolution to match a resolution supported by the TV.
2. Confirm that the correct input source has been selected by selecting Source on the TV.
3. If using an HDMI connection, check the audio output setting on your PC.
4. If using an HDMI-to-DVI cable, make sure a separate audio cable is connected as the HDMI (DVI) port does not transmit audio.
5. To listen to computer sound, connect external speakers to the audio output connection of the computer.

Following these steps should help resolve the issue of 'Mode not supported' appearing on the screen.

question: Schedule Recording is not working. How can I fix this issue?
documents: The Schedule Recording/Timeshift Function Isn't Working. When Timeshift or Schedule recording aren't working, these steps may help resolve the problem. The problem Try this! Schedule Recording cannot be used. This function is not available in the U.S.A. and Canada. This function may not be supported depending on the model or geographical area. Make sure the TV has a network connection. Settings General Network Network Status Contact your Internet service provider. Position your wireless router, modem router, or access point in a central location. Avoid putting it in a corner. Use a wireless repeater to get an instant boost in your wireless signal strength. Place the repeater halfway between your wireless router and your TV. Check if there is a USB device connected to the TV. Recording will automatically stop if the signal becomes too weak. Check the free space on the USB device. The function will not work if there isn't enough storage space on the USB device.
response:
To troubleshoot Schedule Recording not working, you can try the following steps:

1. Ensure that your TV has a network connection by checking the network status and contacting your Internet service provider if needed.
2. Position your wireless router, modem router, or access point in a central location to improve signal strength.
3. Check if there is a USB device connected to the TV and make sure there is enough free space on the USB device for recording.
4. Set up the TV's clock before trying to use the Schedule Recording function.
5. Make sure you are within a supported geographical area for the Schedule Recording function.
6. For optimal functionality, use a USB hard drive with a speed of 5,400 rpm or above, as RAID-type USB hard drives are not supported.
7. Ensure that there is at least 100 MB of free space on the USB storage device for Schedule Recording to work properly.
8. If the available storage space falls below 500 MB while both Schedule Recording and Timeshift functions are in progress, only the recording will stop.
9. Check that the input signal is not changed while recording is in progress, as this can lead to recording interruptions.
10. Be aware that the actual recording may start a second or two later than the specified time when using the Record or Schedule Recording function.

By following these steps and ensuring your setup meets the specified requirements, you can troubleshoot and potentially resolve issues with Schedule Recording not working.

question: How do I fix black and white issue ?
documents: Setting the screen to black and white. Settings General Accessibility Grayscale Try Now You can change the color of the TV screen to black and white to sharpen blurred edges caused by colors. Grayscale is on, some Accessibility menus are not available. This function may not be supported depending on the model or geographical area.
response:
To fix the black and white issue on your TV, you can follow these steps:

1. Go to Settings -> General -> Accessibility -> Grayscale and ensure that the Grayscale option is turned off. This will change the color of the TV screen back to its normal settings.

2. If the issue persists, go to Settings -> Support -> Self Diagnosis -> Start Picture Test. If the test results indicate that the problem is not caused by the TV, check the connections of the external devices connected to the TV. Confirm that the video input connectors are connected properly.

3. If the colors on your TV screen are still black and white or off, try adjusting the Picture Mode settings. Go to Settings -> Picture -> Picture Mode and adjust the settings for Brightness, Sharpness, and Color.

4. If the issue is still not resolved, you can try resetting the picture settings. Go to Settings -> Picture Expert Settings -> Reset Picture.

5. Lastly, check if the Grayscale option is set to on in the Accessibility settings. If it is on, turn it off by going to Settings -> General -> Accessibility -> Grayscale. 

Following these steps should help resolve the black and white issue on your TV.

question: How do I fix Screen Brightness issues?
documents: Reducing the energy consumption of the TV. Settings General Eco Solution Try Now You can adjust the brightness level of the TV, reduce overall power consumption, and prevent overheating. Ambient Light Detection Try Now Automatically adjusts the brightness level of the TV, according to the ambient light level, to reduce power consumption. Motion Lighting Try Now Adjusts the brightness in response to on-screen movements to reduce power consumption. Auto Power Off Try Now Automatically turns off the TV to reduce unnecessary power consumption if there is no operation for 4 hours.
response:
To fix screen brightness issues, you can try the following steps:
1. Disable Ambient Light Detection, Power Saving Mode, or Motion Lighting in the General Eco Solution settings if your TV is flickering or dimming sporadically.
2. Run the Start Picture Test in Support Self Diagnosis settings to determine if the color on your TV screen is correct or if there are any issues.
3. Adjust the brightness settings in Picture Expert Settings, including Backlight, Contrast, Brightness, Sharpness, Color, and Tint (G/R), if needed.
4. Use the Auto Motion Plus Settings function in Picture Expert Settings if you notice ghosting or blurring on the screen.
5. If the picture is not bright enough or the color is poor, check if Power Saving Mode is enabled in the General Eco Solution settings and try resetting the picture in Picture Expert Settings.
6. If using HDMI devices connected to the TV, adjust the black level using the HDMI Black Level function in External Device Manager.

question: How to remove channels from favorites list?
documents: Removing channels from a favorites list. Select channels in a favorites list, and then select the icon.
response:
To remove channels from a favorites list, you need to follow these steps:

1. Open the Channel List.
2. Press the right directional button to move to the Category List.
3. Use the up and down directional buttons to move to "Favorites", and then press Select.
4. Select the favorites list from which you want to delete channels.
5. Use the up directional button to move to "Edit Favorites", and then press Select.
6. In the Edit Favorites screen, move to the channel you want to delete from the favorites list, and then press the Select button.
7. Repeat Step 5 to select all channels you want to delete.
8. Press the left directional button to move to the icon, and then press the Select button.
9. The selected channels will be deleted from the favorites list.

question: What are the steps to connect to the Internet network?
documents: Connecting to your Internet network. Settings General Network Open Network Settings Try Now Connect to an available network. Establishing a wired Internet connection Settings General Network Open Network Settings Wired If you connect a LAN cable, the TV automatically accesses the Internet. Use a CAT 7 (*STP type) cable for the connection. * Shielded Twist Pair The TV will not be able to connect to the Internet if your network speed is below 10 Mbps. LAN  Establishing a wireless Internet connection Settings General Network Open Network Settings Wireless Make sure that you have the wireless router's SSID (name) and password settings before attempting to connect, and then follow the directions on the screen. The image on your TV may differ from the image above depending on the model and geographical area. If no wireless router is found, select Add Network at the bottom of the list and enter the network name (SSID). If your wireless router has a WPS or PBC button, select Use WPS at the bottom of the list, and then push the WPS or PBC button on your router within 2 minutes. The TV will connect automatically. Checking the Internet connection status Settings General Network Network Status Try Now View the current network and Internet status. Wireless Connection Select your wireless network. Refresh
response:
1. Establishing a wired Internet connection: Use a CAT 7 (STP type) cable to connect the TV to the Internet. If your network speed is below 10 Mbps, the TV will not be able to connect to the Internet.

2. Establishing a wireless Internet connection: Make sure you have the wireless router's SSID and password settings. Follow the on-screen directions to connect to a wireless network. If no wireless router is found, you can manually enter the network name (SSID) or use the WPS feature if your router has it.

3. Troubleshooting Internet connectivity issues: If you encounter any problems with the connection, such as no network cable found or wireless network connection failed, follow the troubleshooting steps provided. Make sure the router is turned on, check IP settings, and ensure the DHCP server is active. If the issue persists, contact your Internet Service Provider for assistance.

question: Please instruct how to record any program and Is there any way to stop the recording?
documents: Using the instant and schedule recording options from the guide screen:. To record a program that is on now, open the program guide, move to the program, and then press and hold the Select button. You will get a pop-up menu. Use the up and down directional buttons to move to Record . Press select to record the program. You will be returned to the program guide. Recording automatically ends at the time when the program ends. To open the Guide and stop recording the program, press and hold the Select button. The menu is displayed and there is now a Stop Recording option instead of a Record option. Move to the Stop Recording option, and then press the Select button. To record a program that is on later, move to the program in the Guide , and then press Select. You will get a pop-up menu. Use the up and down directional button to move within this menu. Move to the Schedule Recording , and then press Select. You will hear a message saying the program has been set to record and then you will be returned to the program guide. The icon is placed visually next to the program title to show it is set to record. To find out if a program is set to record or to cancel a scheduled recording, press select on the program. If it is already set to record, then the option on the menu will be Cancel Scheduled Recording . You can press the Select button to cancel the scheduled recording or just press the button to go back to the program guide without cancelling the recording.
response:
To record any program, you can use the instant recording option or the schedule recording option from the guide screen.

1. Instant Recording: Select a live program from the Guide, press and hold the Select button, and then select Record from the pop-up menu to start recording immediately.

2. Schedule Recording: Select a program scheduled to be broadcast from the Guide, press the Select button, and then select Schedule Recording to set up a scheduled recording of the program.

To stop the recording, you can open the Guide while the program is being recorded and press and hold the Select button. A menu will be displayed with the option to Stop Recording. Move to the Stop Recording option and press the Select button to stop the recording.

question: The captions in the TV is grayed out. How should I fix it?
documents: Showing captions. Settings General Accessibility Caption Settings Try Now Set Caption to watch programs with the captions displayed. Captions are not displayed by programs that do not support captions. The TV cannot control or modify DVD or Blu-ray subtitles. To control DVD or Blu-ray subtitles, use the sub-title feature of the DVD or Blu-ray player and the player's remote control. Selecting the caption language Settings General Accessibility Caption Settings Caption Mode Default CC1 CC4 Text1 Text4 (Analog channels only) Operates in either analog TV channel mode or when a signal is supplied from an external device to the TV. (Depending on the broadcast signal, the analog caption function may or may not work with digital channels.) Default Service1 Service6 CC1 CC4 Text1 Text4 (Digital channels only) Works with digital channels. Service1 Service6 may not be available in digital caption mode depending on the broadcast. Setting the digital caption related options Settings General Accessibility Caption Settings Digital Caption Options You can change the font color, background color, and size settings, etc. The foreground and background colors and opacity settings cannot be the same.
response:
To fix the issue of grayed out captions on your TV, you can try the following steps:

1. Go to Settings.
2. Select General.
3. Choose Accessibility.
4. Select Caption Settings.
5. Check the Caption Mode settings to ensure it is set to the desired option (Default, CC1, CC4, Text1, Text4).
6. If the Caption Mode is set to Digital channels only, switch to Analog channels only to see if the captions are enabled.
7. Remember that some channels may not have caption data, so make sure you are on a channel that supports captions.
8. If these steps do not resolve the issue, you may need to adjust the Digital Caption Options such as font color, background color, and size settings.

By following these steps, you should be able to enable the captions on your TV and resolve the issue of grayed out captions.

question: How do I fix unwanted powering off issue?

By checking and disabling these energy-saving functions, you can prevent the TV from turning off unexpectedly.
